description: Comprehensive order management and reporting pipeline that processes customer orders, calculates revenue metrics, manages inventory, generates customer analytics, and produces executive dashboards. The pipeline handles order lifecycle from staging through fulfillment, computes customer lifetime value and engagement scores, tracks product performance, and maintains data quality through validation and archival processes.

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: Foreign key to customer who placed the order

columns.name: order_date
columns.description: Date when the order was placed

columns.name: status
columns.description: Current order status (confirmed, cancelled, delivered, returned, pending)

columns.name: channel
columns.description: Sales channel where order originated (web, mobile, store)

columns.name: payment_method
columns.description: Payment method used for the order

columns.name: shipping_cost
columns.description: Cost of shipping for the order

columns.name: external_ref
columns.description: External reference ID for order tracking

columns.name: created_at
columns.description: Timestamp when order record was created

columns.name: item_id
columns.description: Unique identifier for each order line item

columns.name: order_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the parent order

columns.name: product_id
columns.description: Foreign key to the product being ordered

columns.name: unit_price
columns.description: Price per unit at time of order

columns.name: discount_amount
columns.description: Total discount applied to this line item

columns.name: return_amount
columns.description: Amount refunded if item was returned

columns.name: signup_date
columns.description: Date when customer account was created

columns.name: cost_price
columns.description: Cost of goods sold per unit

columns.name: tax_rate
columns.description: Tax rate percentage for the state

columns.name: region
columns.description: Geographic region for shipping and reporting

columns.name: raw_id
columns.description: Raw order identifier from external system

columns.name: order_date_text
columns.description: Order date as text string from raw feed

columns.name: processed
columns.description: Flag indicating if record has been processed

columns.name: item_count
columns.description: Total number of line items in order

columns.name: subtotal
columns.description: Sum of all line item amounts before tax and shipping

columns.name: grand_total
columns.description: Final order total including all charges

columns.name: etl_timestamp
columns.description: When this summary record was created

columns.name: report_date
columns.description: Date for which revenue is reported

columns.name: total_orders
columns.description: Total number of orders placed by customer

columns.name: months_active
columns.description: Number of months between first and last order

columns.name: last_order_date
columns.description: Date of customer's most recent order

columns.name: predicted_annual_value
columns.description: Projected annual spending based on historical patterns

columns.name: updated_at
columns.description: When LTV record was last updated

columns.name: is_low_stock
columns.description: Flag indicating if stock is below reorder point

columns.name: days_of_supply
columns.description: Estimated days until stock depletion based on sales velocity

columns.name: proc_name
columns.description: Name of stored procedure that executed

columns.name: row_count
columns.description: Number of rows affected by the procedure

columns.name: priority_score
columns.description: Calculated priority score for shipping order

columns.name: queued_at
columns.description: When order was added to shipping queue

columns.name: gross_margin
columns.description: Gross profit (revenue minus cost of goods)

columns.name: margin_pct
columns.description: Gross margin as percentage of revenue

columns.name: kpi_month
columns.description: Month for which KPIs are calculated

columns.name: avg_order_value
columns.description: Average order value for the month

columns.name: active_customers
columns.description: Number of customers who placed orders

columns.name: returning_customers
columns.description: Number of customers with previous orders

columns.name: updated_at
columns.description: When KPI record was last updated

columns.name: raw_order_id
columns.description: Original order ID from source system

columns.name: is_valid
columns.description: Flag indicating if record passed validation

columns.name: promoted
columns.description: Flag indicating if record was promoted to production

columns.name: engagement_label
columns.description: Engagement category (Highly Engaged, Moderately Engaged, Low Engagement)

columns.name: customer_id
columns.description: Customer who placed the archived order

components.name: usp_RefreshOrderSummary
components.description: Rebuilds denormalized order summary by joining orders with customers, order items, tax rates, addresses, and shipping providers. Calculates item counts, subtotals, discounts, taxes, and grand totals. Filters out cancelled orders and processes date range (default last 90 days). Uses DELETE + INSERT pattern for data refresh.
components.sql_text: SELECT o.order_id, o.customer_id, c.first_name + ' ' + c.last_name AS customer_name, c.email AS customer_email, o.order_date, o.status AS order_status, COUNT(oi.item_id) AS item_count, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS subtotal, SUM(oi.discount_amount) AS discount_total, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * tr.tax_rate / 100) AS tax_total, o.shipping_cost AS shipping_total, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) - SUM(oi.discount_amount) + SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * tr.tax_rate / 100) + o.shipping_cost AS grand_total, o.payment_method, o.shipping_method, DATEADD(DAY, sp.avg_delivery_days, o.order_date) AS estimated_delivery, ca.region, GETDATE() AS etl_timestamp FROM dbo.Orders o INNER JOIN dbo.Customers c ON o.customer_id = c.customer_id INNER JOIN dbo.OrderItems oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id LEFT JOIN dbo.TaxRates tr ON c.state = tr.state LEFT JOIN dbo.CustomerAddresses ca ON c.customer_id = ca.customer_id AND ca.address_type = 'shipping' LEFT JOIN dbo.ShippingProviders sp ON o.shipping_method = sp.provider_name WHERE o.order_date BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate AND o.status <> 'cancelled' GROUP BY o.order_id, o.customer_id, c.first_name, c.last_name, c.email, o.order_date, o.status, o.shipping_cost, o.payment_method, o.shipping_method, sp.avg_delivery_days, ca.region

components.name: temp_table_DailyCalc
components.description: Temporary table that aggregates daily revenue metrics by region and channel. Calculates order counts, total revenue, average order values, and counts new customers who signed up on the report date. Groups data by region (defaulting 'Unknown' for missing) and sales channel.
components.sql_text: SELECT @ReportDate AS report_date, COALESCE(ca.region, 'Unknown') AS region, o.channel, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count, SUM(os.grand_total) AS revenue, AVG(os.grand_total) AS avg_order_value, COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN CAST(c.signup_date AS DATE) = @ReportDate THEN c.customer_id END) AS new_customers FROM dbo.OrderSummary os INNER JOIN dbo.Orders o ON os.order_id = o.order_id INNER JOIN dbo.Customers c ON os.customer_id = c.customer_id LEFT JOIN dbo.CustomerAddresses ca ON c.customer_id = ca.customer_id AND ca.address_type = 'billing' WHERE os.order_date = @ReportDate GROUP BY COALESCE(ca.region, 'Unknown'), o.channel

components.name: usp_CalculateDailyRevenue
components.description: Processes daily revenue aggregation using temporary table for intermediate calculations, then merges results into permanent DailyRevenue table. Updates existing records or inserts new ones based on report_date, region, and channel combination. Tracks order counts, revenue totals, average order values, and new customer acquisitions.
components.sql_text: MERGE dbo.DailyRevenue AS tgt USING #DailyCalc AS src ON tgt.report_date = src.report_date AND tgt.region = src.region AND tgt.channel = src.channel WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET tgt.order_count = src.order_count, tgt.revenue = src.revenue, tgt.avg_order_value = src.avg_order_value, tgt.new_customers = src.new_customers, tgt.updated_at = GETDATE() WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (report_date, region, channel, order_count, revenue, avg_order_value, new_customers, created_at) VALUES (src.report_date, src.region, src.channel, src.order_count, src.revenue, src.avg_order_value, src.new_customers, GETDATE())

components.name: temp_table_LTV_Calc
components.description: Temporary table that calculates comprehensive customer lifetime value metrics including total orders, total spend, average order value, months active, days dormant, predicted annual value based on historical patterns, and decile ranking using NTILE window function. Excludes cancelled orders from calculations.
components.sql_text: SELECT c.customer_id, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS total_orders, SUM(os.grand_total) AS total_spend, AVG(os.grand_total) AS avg_order_value, DATEDIFF(MONTH, MIN(o.order_date), MAX(o.order_date)) AS months_active, MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order_date, DATEDIFF(DAY, MAX(o.order_date), GETDATE()) AS days_dormant, CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(MONTH, MIN(o.order_date), MAX(o.order_date)) = 0 THEN SUM(os.grand_total) * 12 ELSE SUM(os.grand_total) / DATEDIFF(MONTH, MIN(o.order_date), MAX(o.order_date)) * 12 END AS predicted_annual_value, NTILE(10) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(os.grand_total) DESC) AS decile_rank FROM dbo.Customers c INNER JOIN dbo.Orders o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id INNER JOIN dbo.OrderSummary os ON o.order_id = os.order_id WHERE o.status <> 'cancelled' GROUP BY c.customer_id

components.name: usp_UpdateCustomerLTV
components.description: Updates customer lifetime value metrics by calculating order history, spending patterns, activity periods, and predictive annual values. Uses temporary table for complex calculations including months active, dormancy periods, and decile rankings. Merges results into permanent CustomerLifetimeValue table.
components.sql_text: MERGE dbo.CustomerLifetimeValue AS tgt USING #LTV_Calc AS src ON tgt.customer_id = src.customer_id WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET tgt.total_orders = src.total_orders, tgt.total_spend = src.total_spend, tgt.avg_order_value = src.avg_order_value, tgt.months_active = src.months_active, tgt.last_order_date = src.last_order_date, tgt.days_dormant = src.days_dormant, tgt.predicted_annual_value = src.predicted_annual_value, tgt.decile_rank = src.decile_rank, tgt.updated_at = GETDATE() WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (customer_id, total_orders, total_spend, avg_order_value, months_active, last_order_date, days_dormant, predicted_annual_value, decile_rank, created_at) VALUES (src.customer_id, src.total_orders, src.total_spend, src.avg_order_value, src.months_active, src.last_order_date, src.days_dormant, src.predicted_annual_value, src.decile_rank, GETDATE())

components.name: subquery_sales_30d
components.description: Calculates 30-day trailing sales velocity by product to estimate daily average sales. Used for inventory days-of-supply calculations. Excludes cancelled and returned orders from velocity calculations.
components.sql_text: SELECT oi.product_id, SUM(oi.quantity) / 30.0 AS daily_avg FROM dbo.OrderItems oi INNER JOIN dbo.Orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, @SnapshotDate) AND o.status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'returned') GROUP BY oi.product_id

components.name: usp_SnapshotInventory
components.description: Creates nightly inventory snapshots with low-stock flagging and days-of-supply calculations. Joins inventory data with products and 30-day sales velocity to calculate available quantities, reorder point comparisons, and estimated days until stock depletion based on historical sales patterns.
components.sql_text: SELECT @SnapshotDate, p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category, i.warehouse_id, i.quantity_on_hand, i.quantity_reserved, i.quantity_on_hand - i.quantity_reserved AS quantity_available, i.reorder_point, CASE WHEN (i.quantity_on_hand - i.quantity_reserved) <= i.reorder_point THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS is_low_stock, CASE WHEN COALESCE(sales_30d.daily_avg, 0) = 0 THEN 9999 ELSE (i.quantity_on_hand - i.quantity_reserved) / sales_30d.daily_avg END AS days_of_supply, i.last_restock_date FROM dbo.Inventory i INNER JOIN dbo.Products p ON i.product_id = p.product_id LEFT JOIN (subquery_sales_30d) sales_30d ON p.product_id = sales_30d.product_id

components.name: table_variable_PendingOrders
components.description: Table variable that identifies pending orders and calculates priority scores based on customer tier (from LTV decile ranking), shipping method urgency, and order age. Assigns higher priority to VIP customers (decile 1-2), express shipping, and older orders. Excludes orders already in shipping queue.
components.sql_text: SELECT os.order_id, os.customer_id, os.order_date, os.shipping_method, os.region, os.grand_total, COALESCE(ltv.decile_rank_label, 'Standard') AS customer_tier, CASE WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 2 THEN 30 WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 5 THEN 20 ELSE 10 END + CASE WHEN os.shipping_method = 'express' THEN 20 WHEN os.shipping_method = 'priority' THEN 10 ELSE 0 END + DATEDIFF(DAY, os.order_date, GETDATE()) * 5 AS priority_score FROM dbo.OrderSummary os LEFT JOIN (SELECT customer_id, decile_rank, CASE WHEN decile_rank <= 2 THEN 'VIP' WHEN decile_rank <= 5 THEN 'Premium' ELSE 'Standard' END AS decile_rank_label FROM dbo.CustomerLifetimeValue) ltv ON os.customer_id = ltv.customer_id WHERE os.order_status = 'confirmed' AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dbo.ShippingQueue sq WHERE sq.order_id = os.order_id)

components.name: usp_ProcessShippingQueue
components.description: Processes confirmed orders into shipping queue with intelligent priority scoring. Combines customer value tier (VIP/Premium/Standard based on LTV decile), shipping method urgency (express/priority/standard), and order age to calculate priority scores. Inserts orders into shipping queue ordered by priority for fulfillment optimization.
components.sql_text: INSERT INTO dbo.ShippingQueue (order_id, customer_id, shipping_method, region, grand_total, customer_tier, priority_score, queue_status, queued_at) SELECT order_id, customer_id, shipping_method, region, grand_total, customer_tier, priority_score, 'pending', GETDATE() FROM @PendingOrders ORDER BY priority_score DESC

components.name: subquery_returns
components.description: Calculates return metrics by product for the reporting period, including total return units and refund amounts. Filters for orders with 'returned' status within the specified date range to provide net revenue calculations.
components.sql_text: SELECT oi2.product_id, SUM(oi2.quantity) AS return_units, SUM(oi2.return_amount) AS refund_total FROM dbo.OrderItems oi2 INNER JOIN dbo.Orders o2 ON oi2.order_id = o2.order_id WHERE o2.status = 'returned' AND o2.order_date BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate GROUP BY oi2.product_id

components.name: usp_RevenueByProduct
components.description: Generates comprehensive product-level revenue analysis including units sold, gross revenue, cost of goods, gross margins, margin percentages, average selling prices, return impacts, and product rankings. Calculates net revenue after returns and provides both revenue-based and unit-based rankings using window functions.
components.sql_text: SELECT @StartDate, @EndDate, p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category, SUM(oi.quantity) AS units_sold, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) AS gross_revenue, SUM(oi.quantity * p.cost_price) AS cost_of_goods, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) - SUM(oi.quantity * p.cost_price) AS gross_margin, CASE WHEN SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE (SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) - SUM(oi.quantity * p.cost_price)) / SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) * 100 END AS margin_pct, AVG(oi.unit_price) AS avg_selling_price, COALESCE(ret.return_units, 0) AS return_units, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) - COALESCE(ret.refund_total, 0) AS net_revenue, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) DESC) AS rank_by_revenue, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity) DESC) AS rank_by_units FROM dbo.OrderItems oi INNER JOIN dbo.Orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id INNER JOIN dbo.Products p ON oi.product_id = p.product_id LEFT JOIN (subquery_returns) ret ON p.product_id = ret.product_id WHERE o.order_date BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate AND o.status NOT IN ('cancelled') GROUP BY p.product_id, p.product_name, p.category, ret.return_units, ret.refund_total

components.name: temp_table_KPI_Current
components.description: Temporary table that aggregates monthly KPI metrics including total revenue, order counts, average order values, new customer acquisitions, month-over-month growth calculations, active customer counts, and returning customer analysis. Compares current month performance against previous month for growth percentage calculations.
components.sql_text: SELECT @MonthStart AS kpi_month, SUM(dr.revenue) AS total_revenue, SUM(dr.order_count) AS total_orders, SUM(dr.revenue) / NULLIF(SUM(dr.order_count), 0) AS avg_order_value, SUM(dr.new_customers) AS new_customers, prev.prev_month_revenue, CASE WHEN prev.prev_month_revenue = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (SUM(dr.revenue) - prev.prev_month_revenue) / prev.prev_month_revenue * 100 END AS mom_growth_pct, (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) FROM dbo.Orders WHERE order_date BETWEEN @MonthStart AND @MonthEnd AND status <> 'cancelled') AS active_customers, (SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT o1.customer_id) FROM dbo.Orders o1 WHERE o1.order_date BETWEEN @MonthStart AND @MonthEnd AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dbo.Orders o2 WHERE o2.customer_id = o1.customer_id AND o2.order_date < @MonthStart)) AS returning_customers FROM dbo.DailyRevenue dr CROSS JOIN (SELECT SUM(revenue) AS prev_month_revenue FROM dbo.DailyRevenue WHERE report_date BETWEEN @PrevMonthStart AND @PrevMonthEnd) prev WHERE dr.report_date BETWEEN @MonthStart AND @MonthEnd

components.name: usp_MonthlyKPIDashboard
components.description: Generates executive dashboard KPIs for specified month including revenue totals, order metrics, customer acquisition and retention analysis, and month-over-month growth calculations. Uses complex subqueries to identify active and returning customers, then merges results into permanent MonthlyKPI table.
components.sql_text: MERGE dbo.MonthlyKPI AS tgt USING #KPI_Current AS src ON tgt.kpi_month = src.kpi_month WHEN MATCHED THEN UPDATE SET tgt.total_revenue = src.total_revenue, tgt.total_orders = src.total_orders, tgt.avg_order_value = src.avg_order_value, tgt.new_customers = src.new_customers, tgt.mom_growth_pct = src.mom_growth_pct, tgt.active_customers = src.active_customers, tgt.returning_customers = src.returning_customers, tgt.updated_at = GETDATE() WHEN NOT MATCHED THEN INSERT (kpi_month, total_revenue, total_orders, avg_order_value, new_customers, mom_growth_pct, active_customers, returning_customers, created_at) VALUES (src.kpi_month, src.total_revenue, src.total_orders, src.avg_order_value, src.new_customers, src.mom_growth_pct, src.active_customers, src.returning_customers, GETDATE())

components.name: usp_StageIncomingOrders
components.description: Loads and validates raw order data into staging with comprehensive data cleansing and validation. Performs email trimming and lowercasing, SKU uppercasing, date parsing, currency and channel defaulting. Validates data quality including date formats, positive quantities and prices, required fields. Concatenates validation errors and flags invalid records.
components.sql_text: INSERT INTO staging.OrderStaging (batch_id, raw_order_id, customer_email, product_sku, quantity, unit_price, order_date_str, order_date_parsed, currency, channel, is_valid, validation_errors, staged_at) SELECT @BatchId, ro.raw_id, LOWER(TRIM(ro.email)), UPPER(TRIM(ro.sku)), ro.qty, ro.price, ro.order_date_text, TRY_CAST(ro.order_date_text AS DATE), UPPER(COALESCE(ro.currency, 'USD')), LOWER(COALESCE(ro.channel, 'web')), CASE WHEN TRY_CAST(ro.order_date_text AS DATE) IS NULL THEN 0 WHEN ro.qty <= 0 THEN 0 WHEN ro.price < 0 THEN 0 WHEN ro.email IS NULL OR ro.email = '' THEN 0 WHEN ro.sku IS NULL OR ro.sku = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END, CONCAT_WS('; ', CASE WHEN TRY_CAST(ro.order_date_text AS DATE) IS NULL THEN 'Invalid date: ' + COALESCE(ro.order_date_text, 'NULL') END, CASE WHEN ro.qty <= 0 THEN 'Invalid quantity' END, CASE WHEN ro.price < 0 THEN 'Negative price' END, CASE WHEN ro.email IS NULL OR ro.email = '' THEN 'Missing email' END, CASE WHEN ro.sku IS NULL OR ro.sku = '' THEN 'Missing SKU' END), GETDATE() FROM staging.RawOrderFeed ro WHERE ro.processed = 0

components.name: usp_PromoteStaging
components.description: Promotes validated staging records into production tables by inserting new orders and order items. Matches customers by email, products by SKU, and applies promotional discounts where applicable. Uses transaction management to ensure data consistency during the promotion process from staging to production.
components.sql_text: INSERT INTO dbo.Orders (customer_id, order_date, status, channel, payment_method, shipping_method, shipping_cost, created_at) SELECT c.customer_id, s.order_date_parsed, 'pending', s.channel, 'credit_card', 'standard', 0.00, GETDATE() FROM staging.OrderStaging s INNER JOIN dbo.Customers c ON s.customer_email = c.email WHERE s.batch_id = @BatchId AND s.is_valid = 1 AND NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dbo.Orders o WHERE o.external_ref = s.raw_order_id)

components.name: cte_CustomerBase
components.description: Base CTE that retrieves active customer information including full name concatenation and tenure calculation in days since signup. Filters for active customers and optionally for specific customer ID when provided as parameter.
components.sql_text: SELECT c.customer_id, c.first_name + ' ' + c.last_name AS customer_name, c.email, c.signup_date, DATEDIFF(DAY, c.signup_date, GETDATE()) AS tenure_days FROM dbo.Customers c WHERE (@CustomerId IS NULL OR c.customer_id = @CustomerId) AND c.status = 'active'

components.name: cte_OrderMetrics
components.description: CTE that calculates order-level metrics for each customer including total order count, total spend, average order value, and last order date. Excludes cancelled and returned orders from calculations to provide accurate customer value metrics.
components.sql_text: SELECT cb.customer_id, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS order_count, SUM(os.grand_total) AS total_spend, AVG(os.grand_total) AS avg_order, MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order FROM CustomerBase cb LEFT JOIN dbo.Orders o ON cb.customer_id = o.customer_id LEFT JOIN dbo.OrderSummary os ON o.order_id = os.order_id WHERE o.status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'returned') GROUP BY cb.customer_id

components.name: cte_EngagementScore
components.description: CTE that calculates customer engagement scores (0-100) based on recency (last order within 30/90/180 days), frequency (order count tiers), and customer value (LTV decile ranking). Combines multiple engagement factors with weighted scoring to produce overall engagement assessment.
components.sql_text: SELECT cb.customer_id, LEAST(100, (CASE WHEN om.last_order IS NULL THEN 0 WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, om.last_order, GETDATE()) <= 30 THEN 40 WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, om.last_order, GETDATE()) <= 90 THEN 25 WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, om.last_order, GETDATE()) <= 180 THEN 10 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN om.order_count >= 20 THEN 30 WHEN om.order_count >= 10 THEN 20 WHEN om.order_count >= 3 THEN 10 ELSE 5 END) + (CASE WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 2 THEN 30 WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 5 THEN 20 ELSE 10 END)) AS engagement_score FROM CustomerBase cb LEFT JOIN OrderMetrics om ON cb.customer_id = om.customer_id LEFT JOIN dbo.CustomerLifetimeValue ltv ON cb.customer_id = ltv.customer_id

components.name: usp_GenerateCustomerScorecard
components.description: Generates comprehensive customer scorecards combining customer demographics, order history, lifetime value predictions, and engagement scoring. Uses multiple CTEs to build layered customer analytics including tenure, spending patterns, predicted annual value, and engagement labels (Highly Engaged, Moderately Engaged, Low Engagement) based on calculated scores.
components.sql_text: MERGE dbo.CustomerScorecard AS tgt USING (SELECT cb.customer_id, cb.customer_name, cb.email, cb.signup_date, cb.tenure_days, COALESCE(om.order_count, 0) AS order_count, COALESCE(om.total_spend, 0) AS total_spend, COALESCE(om.avg_order, 0) AS avg_order, om.last_order, COALESCE(ltv.predicted_annual_value, 0) AS predicted_annual_value, ltv.decile_rank, es.engagement_score, CASE WHEN es.engagement_score >= 70 THEN 'Highly Engaged' WHEN es.engagement_score >= 40 THEN 'Moderately Engaged' ELSE 'Low Engagement' END AS engagement_label, GETDATE() AS scored_at FROM CustomerBase cb LEFT JOIN OrderMetrics om ON cb.customer_id = om.customer_id LEFT JOIN dbo.CustomerLifetimeValue ltv ON cb.customer_id = ltv.customer_id LEFT JOIN EngagementScore es ON cb.customer_id = es.customer_id) AS src ON tgt.customer_id = src.customer_id

components.name: usp_NightlyETLOrchestrator
components.description: Master orchestrator procedure that executes the complete nightly ETL pipeline in sequence. Calls 8 sub-procedures including order summary refresh, daily revenue calculation, customer LTV updates, inventory snapshots, shipping queue processing, product revenue analysis, monthly KPI generation, and customer scorecard updates. Provides centralized execution control for the entire pipeline.
components.sql_text: EXEC dbo.usp_RefreshOrderSummary @StartDate = @Today, @EndDate = @Today; EXEC dbo.usp_CalculateDailyRevenue @ReportDate = @Today; EXEC dbo.usp_UpdateCustomerLTV; EXEC dbo.usp_SnapshotInventory @SnapshotDate = @Today; EXEC dbo.usp_ProcessShippingQueue; EXEC dbo.usp_RevenueByProduct @StartDate = DATEFROMPARTS(@Year, @Month, 1), @EndDate = @Today; EXEC dbo.usp_MonthlyKPIDashboard @Month = @Month, @Year = @Year; EXEC dbo.usp_GenerateCustomerScorecard @CustomerId = NULL

components.name: usp_CleanupOldData
components.description: Archives and purges old records based on configurable retention periods. Archives completed orders (delivered, returned, cancelled) older than retention threshold to archive schema, then deletes them from production. Also cleans up old audit logs (1 year retention) and inventory snapshots (6 months retention) to manage database size and performance.
components.sql_text: INSERT INTO archive.Orders SELECT *, GETDATE() AS archived_at FROM dbo.Orders WHERE order_date < @CutoffDate AND status IN ('delivered', 'returned', 'cancelled'); DELETE oi FROM dbo.OrderItems oi INNER JOIN dbo.Orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.order_date < @CutoffDate AND o.status IN ('delivered', 'returned', 'cancelled'); DELETE FROM dbo.Orders WHERE order_date < @CutoffDate AND status IN ('delivered', 'returned', 'cancelled')

column_lineage.transformation: o.order_id
column_lineage.notes: Direct copy of order identifier from source orders table

column_lineage.transformation: c.first_name + ' ' + c.last_name
column_lineage.notes: Concatenates customer first and last name with space separator for display

column_lineage.transformation: COUNT(oi.item_id)
column_lineage.notes: Counts total number of line items in each order

column_lineage.transformation: SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price)
column_lineage.notes: Calculates order subtotal by summing quantity times unit price for all line items

column_lineage.transformation: SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * tr.tax_rate / 100)
column_lineage.notes: Calculates total tax by applying state tax rate to line item amounts

column_lineage.transformation: SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) - SUM(oi.discount_amount) + SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * tr.tax_rate / 100) + o.shipping_cost
column_lineage.notes: Calculates final order total: subtotal minus discounts plus tax plus shipping

column_lineage.transformation: DATEADD(DAY, sp.avg_delivery_days, o.order_date)
column_lineage.notes: Estimates delivery date by adding provider's average delivery days to order date

column_lineage.transformation: COALESCE(ca.region, 'Unknown')
column_lineage.notes: Uses customer billing address region, defaults to 'Unknown' if missing

column_lineage.transformation: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN CAST(c.signup_date AS DATE) = @ReportDate THEN c.customer_id END)

intermediate_steps.expression: COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN CAST(c.signup_date AS DATE) = @ReportDate THEN c.customer_id END)

column_lineage.notes: Counts customers who signed up on the specific report date

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(MONTH, MIN(o.order_date), MAX(o.order_date)) = 0 THEN SUM(os.grand_total) * 12 ELSE SUM(os.grand_total) / DATEDIFF(MONTH, MIN(o.order_date), MAX(o.order_date)) * 12 END

intermediate_steps.expression: CASE WHEN DATEDIFF(MONTH, MIN(o.order_date), MAX(o.order_date)) = 0 THEN SUM(os.grand_total) * 12 ELSE SUM(os.grand_total) / DATEDIFF(MONTH, MIN(o.order_date), MAX(o.order_date)) * 12 END

column_lineage.notes: Predicts annual spending: if single month activity, multiply by 12; otherwise annualize based on months active

column_lineage.transformation: NTILE(10) OVER (ORDER BY SUM(os.grand_total) DESC)
column_lineage.notes: Ranks customers into 10 deciles by total spend (1=top 10%, 10=bottom 10%)

column_lineage.transformation: i.quantity_on_hand - i.quantity_reserved
column_lineage.notes: Calculates available inventory by subtracting reserved quantities from on-hand stock

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN (i.quantity_on_hand - i.quantity_reserved) <= i.reorder_point THEN 1 ELSE 0 END
column_lineage.notes: Flags products as low stock when available quantity falls at or below reorder point

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN COALESCE(sales_30d.daily_avg, 0) = 0 THEN 9999 ELSE (i.quantity_on_hand - i.quantity_reserved) / sales_30d.daily_avg END
column_lineage.notes: Estimates days until stock depletion based on 30-day sales velocity; returns 9999 for zero sales

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 2 THEN 'VIP' WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 5 THEN 'Premium' ELSE 'Standard' END

intermediate_steps.expression: CASE WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 2 THEN 'VIP' WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 5 THEN 'Premium' ELSE 'Standard' END

column_lineage.notes: Assigns customer tier based on LTV decile: top 20% = VIP, next 30% = Premium, rest = Standard

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 2 THEN 30 WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 5 THEN 20 ELSE 10 END + CASE WHEN os.shipping_method = 'express' THEN 20 WHEN os.shipping_method = 'priority' THEN 10 ELSE 0 END + DATEDIFF(DAY, os.order_date, GETDATE()) * 5

intermediate_steps.expression: CASE WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 2 THEN 30 WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 5 THEN 20 ELSE 10 END + CASE WHEN os.shipping_method = 'express' THEN 20 WHEN os.shipping_method = 'priority' THEN 10 ELSE 0 END + DATEDIFF(DAY, os.order_date, GETDATE()) * 5

column_lineage.notes: Calculates shipping priority: customer tier points + shipping method urgency + age penalty (5 points per day)

column_lineage.transformation: SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) - SUM(oi.quantity * p.cost_price)
column_lineage.notes: Calculates gross profit by subtracting total cost of goods from gross revenue

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) = 0 THEN 0 ELSE (SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) - SUM(oi.quantity * p.cost_price)) / SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) * 100 END
column_lineage.notes: Calculates gross margin percentage; handles division by zero with zero revenue

column_lineage.transformation: RANK() OVER (ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price) DESC)
column_lineage.notes: Ranks products by total revenue in descending order (1 = highest revenue)

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN prev.prev_month_revenue = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (SUM(dr.revenue) - prev.prev_month_revenue) / prev.prev_month_revenue * 100 END

intermediate_steps.expression: CASE WHEN prev.prev_month_revenue = 0 THEN NULL ELSE (SUM(dr.revenue) - prev.prev_month_revenue) / prev.prev_month_revenue * 100 END

column_lineage.notes: Calculates month-over-month revenue growth percentage; returns NULL for zero previous month revenue

column_lineage.transformation: COUNT(DISTINCT o1.customer_id) FROM dbo.Orders o1 WHERE o1.order_date BETWEEN @MonthStart AND @MonthEnd AND EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM dbo.Orders o2 WHERE o2.customer_id = o1.customer_id AND o2.order_date < @MonthStart)

intermediate_steps.expression: COUNT(DISTINCT o1.customer_id) WHERE EXISTS previous orders

column_lineage.notes: Counts customers who placed orders in current month and had previous orders before the month started

column_lineage.transformation: LOWER(TRIM(ro.email))
column_lineage.notes: Cleanses email by trimming whitespace and converting to lowercase for consistency

column_lineage.transformation: TRY_CAST(ro.order_date_text AS DATE)
column_lineage.notes: Attempts to parse text date string into DATE type; returns NULL if invalid

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN TRY_CAST(ro.order_date_text AS DATE) IS NULL THEN 0 WHEN ro.qty <= 0 THEN 0 WHEN ro.price < 0 THEN 0 WHEN ro.email IS NULL OR ro.email = '' THEN 0 WHEN ro.sku IS NULL OR ro.sku = '' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END
column_lineage.notes: Validates record quality: requires valid date, positive quantity and price, non-empty email and SKU

column_lineage.transformation: DATEDIFF(DAY, c.signup_date, GETDATE())
column_lineage.notes: Calculates customer tenure as number of days since account signup

column_lineage.transformation: LEAST(100, (CASE WHEN om.last_order IS NULL THEN 0 WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, om.last_order, GETDATE()) <= 30 THEN 40 WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, om.last_order, GETDATE()) <= 90 THEN 25 WHEN DATEDIFF(DAY, om.last_order, GETDATE()) <= 180 THEN 10 ELSE 0 END) + (CASE WHEN om.order_count >= 20 THEN 30 WHEN om.order_count >= 10 THEN 20 WHEN om.order_count >= 3 THEN 10 ELSE 5 END) + (CASE WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 2 THEN 30 WHEN ltv.decile_rank <= 5 THEN 20 ELSE 10 END))
column_lineage.notes: Calculates engagement score (0-100) combining recency (40 pts), frequency (30 pts), and value tier (30 pts)

column_lineage.transformation: CASE WHEN es.engagement_score >= 70 THEN 'Highly Engaged' WHEN es.engagement_score >= 40 THEN 'Moderately Engaged' ELSE 'Low Engagement' END
column_lineage.notes: Categorizes customers: Highly Engaged (70+), Moderately Engaged (40-69), Low Engagement (<40)

data_flow_edges.edge_label: filter: order_date BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate AND status <> 'cancelled'
left join on customer_id AND address_type = 'shipping'
delete existing + insert new for date range
left join on customer_id AND address_type = 'billing'
inner join on customer_id, filter: status <> 'cancelled'
inner join on order_id, filter: order_date >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, @SnapshotDate) AND status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'returned')
left join on product_id for days of supply calculation
filter: order_status = 'confirmed' AND not in shipping queue
left join on customer_id for tier assignment
NOT EXISTS check to exclude already queued orders
inner join on order_id, filter: order_date BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate AND status NOT IN ('cancelled')
inner join on order_id, filter: status = 'returned' AND order_date BETWEEN @StartDate AND @EndDate
left join on product_id for net revenue calculation
delete existing + insert new for period
subqueries for active and returning customer counts
inner join on email for customer matching
inner join on sku for product matching
filter: status = 'active' AND (@CustomerId IS NULL OR customer_id = @CustomerId)
left join on customer_id, filter: status NOT IN ('cancelled', 'returned')
inner join with orders for deletion
